text: The robotic right hemicolectomy procedure is underway, focusing on intracorporeal anastomosis using a V-lock suture to treat sickle adenocarcinoma.
The robotic arms perform a right hemicolectomy, followed by an intracorporeal anastomosis using a V-lock suture to treat sickle adenocarcinoma.
The surgeon uses robotic monopolar curved scissors to score the mesentery, creating a window beneath the identified ileocolic pedicle.

The surgeon mobilizes the remainder of the right colon towards the middle colic vessels using a medial to lateral dissection technique, visibly separating the colon from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon extracts the specimen through a three-centimeter suprapubic incision, completing the removal process.
